plannerOutput.taskSummary: Fix four confirmed bugs in binayah-properties and enhance the communities experience: (1) bathrooms filter mislabeled as bedrooms in SearchPageClient, (2) developer input shows 'community' placeholder, (3) footer Privacy Policy and Terms of Service links are dead (href=#), (4) /communities grid misses all community_info_pages documents — only shows the communities collection.
confirmedScope: FIX 1 — SearchPageClient.tsx: change t('bedrooms') → t('bathrooms') on the bathrooms filter input (~line 260).
FIX 2 — SearchPageClient.tsx: change t('community') → t('developer') on the developer name input placeholder.
FIX 3 — Create /privacy-policy and /terms-of-service stub pages (app router pages) with placeholder translated text. Update Footer.tsx links to real hrefs.

FIX 4 + ENHANCEMENT — CommunitiesPageClient: fetch both /api/communities (communities collection) AND /api/community-info-pages (or direct MongoDB via server component) to build the grid. De-duplicate by slug. Show a combined grid card for every unique slug present in either source.

ENHANCEMENT — /communities/[slug] detail page: merge DB data (project list from communities collection) with Wikipedia/scraped data (description, hero image, stats from community_info_pages). If both exist → merge into one rich page. If only community_info_pages data → show that. If only communities DB data → show projects list. The page should be visually beautiful: hero image, description, key stats, then project cards.

plan:
## Why this will be good
Four confirmed bugs killed in one diff with zero scope creep. The communities upgrade is the headline win: today /communities shows ~10 cards from one collection while community_info_pages (Wikipedia-enriched docs scraped at runtime) sit invisible. After this change, the grid surfaces every community we know about from either source, deduped by slug, with real Wikipedia hero thumbnails — "show the data, not a symbol of it." The detail page stops being two disjoint experiences (DB-only OR Wikipedia-only) and becomes one merged page: Wikipedia hero + description sit on top, the existing project list sits below, both contextualised. Production-ready: empty/error states for the merged listing fetch, graceful fallback gradient when no hero image exists, mobile-first layout, all strings through t(), ar/en/zh/ru locales updated, no new image domains needed (upload.wikimedia.org already whitelisted). Benchmark: Airbnb's neighbourhood guides — hero + prose + a real grid of bookings underneath, in that order.

## Stage 1 — single ship

### FIX 1 — bathrooms filter label
File: `src/app/search/SearchPageClient.tsx` line 260.
Change `placeholder={t("bedrooms")}` to `placeholder={t("bathrooms")}` on the FilterSelect bound to `baths`. The key `search.bathrooms` already exists in messages/en.json (and ar/zh/ru). Verify all four locale files have it; add if missing.

### FIX 2 — developer input placeholder
File: `src/app/search/SearchPageClient.tsx` line 262.
Change the `<input>` bound to `developer`'s `placeholder={t("community")}` to `placeholder={t("developer")}`. Key `search.developer` already exists at line 316 of messages/en.json. Verify ar/zh/ru also have `developer` under the search namespace; add if missing.

### FIX 3 — footer privacy / terms links + stub pages
**A. Create `src/app/[locale]/privacy-policy/page.tsx`** — server component, mirrors the `[locale]/about/page.tsx` shape:
  - Exports `metadata` with title "Privacy Policy | Binayah Properties" and a one-sentence description.
  - Renders a client component `PrivacyPolicyClient.tsx` (sibling file).
  - Client component must include `<Navbar />`, `<Footer />`, `<WhatsAppButton />`, and a `<Breadcrumbs items={[{ label: t('breadcrumb'), href: '/privacy-policy' }]} />`.
  - Layout: brand-green hero (same gradient pattern used by CommunitiesPageClient — `linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)`) with hero label + title + subtitle, then a `max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 py-16` body containing 3–5 placeholder sections (Introduction, Information We Collect, How We Use Information, Your Rights, Contact). Each section: `h2` + 1–2 paragraphs of generic placeholder copy.
  - **Every visible string must come from `t(...)`. No JSX literals.** Use a new namespace `privacyPolicy` in `messages/en.json` (and ar/zh/ru) with keys: `breadcrumb`, `heroLabel`, `heroTitle`, `heroSubtitle`, `lastUpdated`, then `sections.introduction.title|body`, `sections.dataCollected.title|body`, `sections.dataUse.title|body`, `sections.yourRights.title|body`, `sections.contact.title|body`. Body strings may contain prose (no embedded HTML).

**B. Create `src/app/[locale]/terms-of-service/page.tsx` + `TermsOfServiceClient.tsx`** — same shape, namespace `termsOfService` with the same key structure (substitute relevant section names: `acceptance`, `useOfService`, `intellectualProperty`, `liability`, `governing`).

**C. Update `src/components/Footer.tsx` lines 132–133**:
  - Replace `<a href="#">` with `<Link href={"/privacy-policy"}>` and `<Link href={"/terms-of-service"}>` from `next/link`. Locale prefix is handled by next-intl's middleware so the bare hrefs are correct (this matches existing internal links elsewhere in Footer).

**Translation parity:** add the new `privacyPolicy` and `termsOfService` namespaces to **all four** locale files: `messages/en.json`, `messages/ar.json`, `messages/zh.json`, `messages/ru.json`. Arabic strings should be RTL-correct prose; for zh/ru, provide reasonable translations (DEV may use English copy as-is for those if proper translations aren't available, but the keys must exist in every file or next-intl will throw).

### FIX 4 + ENHANCEMENT — communities listing merges both sources
**A. Server-side merge in `src/app/[locale]/communities/page.tsx`**:
  - Keep the existing Fastify `serverFetch(serverApiUrl("/api/communities"))` call.
  - In parallel, query MongoDB directly via `clientPromise` for `community_info_pages.find({}).toArray()`. Project only the fields the client needs: `slug`, `name`, `heroImage`, `description`, `location`. Wrap in try/catch — DB failure must not break the page; log and continue with `[]`.
  - Build a merged array, deduped by `slug`, that the client receives. Shape:
    ```ts
    type CommunityCard = {
      slug: string;
      name: string;
      description?: string;
      thumbnail?: string;          // best available image
      hasListings: boolean;        // true if present in communities collection
      hasGuide: boolean;           // true if present in community_info_pages
    };
    ```
    Merge rule: when a slug exists in both, prefer `communities` collection's `name` (display polish) and use `imageGallery[0] || featuredImage || community_info_pages.heroImage` for thumbnail; description prefers `community_info_pages.description` (Wikipedia prose) over the DB description (often HTML-stripped marketing copy). When only one source has the slug, use whatever it provides.
  - Strip any embedded HTML from descriptions before sending to client (existing code already does `.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, "")` — apply consistently).
  - All MongoDB ObjectIds and Date fields must be stripped before crossing the RSC boundary (mirror the serialization pattern already used in `[locale]/communities/[slug]/page.tsx`).

**B. Update `src/app/communities/CommunitiesPageClient.tsx`**:
  - Change the `Community` interface to `CommunityCard` matching the server shape.
  - Rendering: same overall hero + grid layout (do not redesign from scratch — the existing aspect-[3/4] image-card pattern is good and matches the design principles). Card must:
    1. Show the thumbnail (real Wikipedia image when only `hasGuide`, real DB image when `hasListings`). Use `FALLBACK_IMAGE` only when neither source provided one.
    2. Show the name and a 2-line description clamp (already clamped to 100 chars via slice — keep, but apply to the merged description).
    3. Render a small pill badge in the top-left when the card is **guide-only** (`!hasListings && hasGuide`): badge text from a new key `communities.badgeGuide` (e.g. "Community guide"). Badge style: small, semi-transparent dark-green background (`bg-foreground/70 text-white`), `rounded-full px-2.5 py-1 text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-wider`, positioned `absolute top-3 left-3 z-10`. **Do not** render a badge when the card has listings — silence is the default state.
    4. Tap target: full card link to `/communities/${slug}` (already does this — same destination for both kinds; the slug page already routes correctly via the existing `community_info_pages`-first lookup).
  - Empty state: when `communities.length === 0`, render a centered card with a `<MapPin>` icon + `t("communities.emptyTitle")` + `t("communities.emptyBody")`. New i18n keys.
  - Add new keys to `communities` namespace in **all four** locale files: `badgeGuide`, `emptyTitle`, `emptyBody`.

### ENHANCEMENT — /communities/[slug] merged detail page
**File: `src/app/[locale]/communities/[slug]/page.tsx`** — rewrite the merge logic:
  - Fetch BOTH sources in parallel (don't early-return on community_info_pages):
    - `community_info_pages` doc by slug from MongoDB (existing code).
    - `getCommunity(slug)` from Fastify (existing code).
  - Decide what to render based on what came back:
    1. **Both present** → merge: pass a unified `community` prop to a new client `CommunityMergedDetailClient` that renders the rich Wikipedia-flavoured layout AND the projects grid below.
    2. **Only community_info_pages** → render existing `CommunityInfoDetailClient` unchanged (current behaviour, preserved).
    3. **Only communities collection** → render existing `CommunityDetailClient` unchanged (current behaviour, preserved).
    4. **Neither** → `notFound()`.
  - Serialize aggressively before crossing the RSC boundary (no ObjectIds, no Dates, no embedded HTML in descriptions).

**New client component: `src/app/communities/[slug]/CommunityMergedDetailClient.tsx`** — this is the headline UI work. Requirements:
  - Reuse the existing `CommunityInfoDetailClient` hero treatment (full-bleed Wikipedia hero image with foreground gradient, breadcrumbs, badge, name, location pill). Do not invent a new hero — match the existing pattern.
  - Below the hero: the existing `CommunityInfoDetailClient` body sections (description, sidebar with location/developer/price range, amenities) — same components, same Tailwind structure. The visual identity must be cohesive with `CommunityInfoDetailClient` so a user navigating between guide-only and merged pages doesn't feel a stylistic break.
  - **New section at the end, before the brand-green CTA banner:** a "Properties in {communityName}" section that renders the projects grid using the same card pattern as the existing `CommunityDetailClient` (project image, status badge, developer name, project name, starting price, completion year). Section title via `t("communityMergedDetail.propertiesTitle")` with project count appended. Empty state: don't render the section at all if `projects.length === 0` — the guide content above already justifies the page.
  - The brand-green CTA banner from `CommunityInfoDetailClient` lives at the bottom unchanged.
  - Layout principle: **show the data, not a symbol of it.** Wikipedia photo > placeholder gradient. Real project cards > generic "contact us" panel.
  - Mobile: every interactive element ≥44px tap target. Hero collapses to `h-[50vh]` on small screens (already the pattern in CommunityInfoDetailClient — copy it).
  - Animations: framer-motion `whileInView` reveals on the body sections (already the pattern). Project cards stagger by `i * 0.05`.
  - **Every visible string via t().** New namespace `communityMergedDetail` with keys: `propertiesTitle` (with `{communityName}` interpolation and `{count}`), `propertiesEyebrow` (e.g. "Available now"). Reuse existing `communityInfo.*` keys for the hero/body sections — do not duplicate them.

### Acceptance criteria (the bar to ship)
1. Search page at /search shows correct labels: "Bathrooms" placeholder on the bathrooms filter, "Developer" placeholder on the developer input.
2. Footer Privacy Policy and Terms of Service links navigate to real pages (no `href="#"`).
3. /privacy-policy and /terms-of-service render fully, with Navbar + Footer, in en/ar/zh/ru locales without next-intl missing-key errors.
4. /communities grid shows MORE cards than before (every unique slug from both sources). Cards from `community_info_pages`-only have a small "Community guide" badge; cards backed by listings show no badge. All cards link to `/communities/{slug}`.
5. /communities/[slug] for a slug that exists in BOTH collections shows: Wikipedia hero image + breadcrumbs + name + location, then description block + sidebar (location/developer/price range) + amenities, then a "Properties in {name}" grid with the project cards from the communities collection, then the brand-green CTA banner. No layout breaks.
6. /communities/[slug] for a guide-only slug renders the existing `CommunityInfoDetailClient` unchanged.
7. /communities/[slug] for a listings-only slug renders the existing `CommunityDetailClient` unchanged.
8. `npm run build` passes with no i18n linter errors (no JSX literal strings).
9. `npm run lint` passes.
10. All four locale files (en.json, ar.json, zh.json, ru.json) contain the new namespaces — `privacyPolicy`, `termsOfService`, additions to `communities`, new `communityMergedDetail`.

### Files DEV will touch
- `src/app/search/SearchPageClient.tsx` (2 one-char-ish edits)
- `src/components/Footer.tsx` (2 anchor tags → Link)
- `src/app/[locale]/privacy-policy/page.tsx` (new)
- `src/app/[locale]/privacy-policy/PrivacyPolicyClient.tsx` (new)
- `src/app/[locale]/terms-of-service/page.tsx` (new)
- `src/app/[locale]/terms-of-service/TermsOfServiceClient.tsx` (new)
- `src/app/[locale]/communities/page.tsx` (rewrite to merge two sources server-side)
- `src/app/communities/CommunitiesPageClient.tsx` (new shape, badge, empty state)
- `src/app/[locale]/communities/[slug]/page.tsx` (rewrite to support merged path)
- `src/app/communities/[slug]/CommunityMergedDetailClient.tsx` (new)
- `messages/en.json`, `messages/ar.json`, `messages/zh.json`, `messages/ru.json` (new keys/namespaces)

### Files DEV must NOT touch
- `src/lib/communityScraper.ts` (out of scope — no scraper changes)
- `src/app/api/community-info/route.ts` (out of scope — no new API behaviour)
- `src/components/CommunityInfoDetailClient.tsx` (preserve as-is for guide-only path)
- `src/app/communities/[slug]/CommunityDetailClient.tsx` (preserve as-is for listings-only path)
- `next.config.ts` (upload.wikimedia.org already whitelisted)
- Any binayah-api code

### Demo path (the click-through proving it works)
1. Open `/en/search` → see the property type / community / bedrooms / **bathrooms** / min-price filters in row 1, and the developer input in slot 6 with placeholder **"Developer"** (not "Community").
2. Scroll to footer → click **Privacy Policy** → land on `/en/privacy-policy` with full Navbar/Footer and styled hero. Back, click **Terms of Service** → same for `/en/terms-of-service`.
3. Open `/en/communities` → grid renders with both DB-backed cards and Wikipedia-guide cards visible. Cards without listings show a "Community guide" badge. Confirm at least one previously-invisible community (one only in `community_info_pages`) now appears.
4. Click a card backed by a community that exists in both collections → land on the merged detail page: Wikipedia hero image, description, sidebar stats, then a "Properties in {name}" grid with real project cards, then the gold CTA button.
5. Click a guide-only card → land on the existing `CommunityInfoDetailClient` (unchanged).
6. Click a listings-only card → land on the existing `CommunityDetailClient` (unchanged).
7. Switch locale to /ar/communities — same behaviour, RTL, no missing-translation flashes.

 export default async function CommunitiesPage() {

+  let wikiCommunities: Array<{

+    heroImage?: string;
+    location?: string;
+  }> = [];
+  try {
+    const client = await clientPromise;
+    const db = client.db("binayah_web_new_dev");
+    const docs = await db
+      .collection("community_info_pages")
+      .find({})
+      .project({ slug: 1, name: 1, description: 1, heroImage: 1, location: 1, _id: 0 })

+  // Build a slug-keyed map from DB entries

+  // Build merged list — DB slugs take precedence
+  const merged: CommunityCard[] = [];
+  const seen = new Set<string>();

+  // First pass: DB communities (may also have a wiki counterpart)

+    merged.push({
